last_fix.description: TRENDING_MIN_HOLD_2H gate upgraded 20% -> 50%. EXIT_AGENT_PROMPT_AMENDMENT (BTC/ETH LONG+TRENDING 3h patience) is now live in prompts.py. Gate upgrade aligns graduated rule with prompt amendment. Exec forensics evidence: wins hold 3.3h vs losses 1.5h (2.2x ratio). 87% noise stops in <2h confirmed.
last_fix.outcome_tracking: PENDING_RESTART — bot offline 600.8h. Gate active for live trading.
last_fix.justification: conf=80 >= 75 threshold. EXIT_AGENT_PROMPT_AMENDMENT now live means the underlying fix is deployed. Gate upgrade expands A/B coverage from 20% to 50% to collect faster graduation data on restart.
last_fix.expected_impact: 50% of trending-regime signals now get min-hold treatment. Combined with prompt amendment, should reduce noise stops by 20-30% in trending regime.

new_findings_this_run.detail: 4 of 8 ideas in meta_learning/ideas.json propose a 1.08x confidence_multiplier BOOST for time windows that have 33% WR (evening: 33% WR n=9, afternoon: 33% WR n=15, night: 33% WR n=9). This is directionally inverted — boosting confidence during losing sessions would increase trade volume exactly when the bot performs worst. Root cause: the idea generator may be applying a fixed 'boost' template regardless of whether the WR is above or below breakeven. Additionally, idea_07f475b8 and idea_623426ad are exact duplicates (same night-boost idea, same parameters, same timestamp).
bad_ideas: idea_2238f358: boost evening (33% WR) — should be PENALIZE or BLOCK
idea_07f475b8: boost night (33% WR) — should be PENALIZE; night_session_block_v1 already exists
idea_920b3e13: boost afternoon (33% WR) — should be PENALIZE
correctly_directioned: idea_f5e1b611: boost morning (67% WR) — CORRECT direction, aligns with morning edge
idea_98fac4ad: side balance enforcement — CORRECT direction (80% LONG, 38% WR LONG)
idea_e5ebabb4: strategy weight rebalancing — neutral, no obvious inversion
new_findings_this_run.action: HUMAN REVIEW: Delete or invert the 4 bad ideas. Fix idea generator to check WR vs 50% breakeven before assigning 'boost'. RISK: if any inverted idea is ever applied, it would harm the bot during its worst trading hours.

new_findings_this_run.detail: meta_learning/insights.json claims 'larger positions (>5.0x) have 57-60% WR vs 31-40% for smaller' (confidence 0.75, n=50) — suggesting bigger is better. execution_forensics.json shows 6-10x leverage = -$0.38/trade NET NEGATIVE despite 41% WR, due to tail losses dominating. This is a WR% vs PnL conflict: high leverage can increase WR through leverage amplification while simultaneously destroying PnL via outsized losses on the losing trades. The meta_learning insight is misleading — WR alone without PnL context understates tail risk at high leverage.
new_findings_this_run.resolution: Trust execution_forensics PnL data over meta_learning WR-only insight. Max 5x leverage cap is correct. The meta_learning idea_f36e9743 (confidence-proportional sizing up to 10x) is partially invalidated: the >85% confidence → 10x tier is contradicted by forensics. Only the 3x and 5x tiers are supported.
new_findings_this_run.action: Add note to insights.json that size-WR insights require PnL cross-validation before application. Do not apply idea_f36e9743 at >85% confidence → 10x tier.

new_findings_this_run.detail: 3 near-identical 'size edge: larger positions >5.0x have X% WR' insights with slightly different WR values (57%, 60%, 58%) at the same confidence (0.75) and evidence_count (50). Indicates the insights accumulator is appending rather than updating on each run. This inflates confidence through repetition rather than new evidence.
new_findings_this_run.action: Deduplicate insights by category+evidence_count+description similarity. Insights accumulator should UPDATE existing entries rather than append.
